dataset.description: Multiple sclerosis lesion segmentation from multimodal brain MRI
dataset.acquisition_info: 1.5T and 3T MRI scanners with T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and FLAIR sequences. FLAIR: mean slice thickness 2.61mm (±0.62), pixel spacing 0.75×0.75mm (±0.32×0.32). T1-w: mean slice thickness 1.14mm (±0.81), pixel spacing 0.91×0.91mm (±0.32). T2-w: mean slice thickness 4.24mm (±0.92), pixel spacing 0.58×0.58mm (±0.27).
dataset.annotation_info: Manual segmentation by a junior rater with validation by two senior experts (neuroradiologist and neurologist). FLAIR was primary modality with cross-checking using T2-w and T1-w sequences. Annotations were reviewed and validated by senior experts to establish ground truth.
dataset.data_source: MSLesSeg dataset, collected from Garibaldi Hospital (Catania, Italy) with additional scans from private clinics. Part of the dataset was used in the ICPR 2024 Competition on Multiple Sclerosis Lesion Segmentation.
dataset.detailed_description: The MSLesSeg dataset is a publicly available brain MRI dataset designed for multiple sclerosis lesion segmentation. It contains 115 multimodal MRI scans from 75 patients, including T1-weighted, T2-weighted, and FLAIR sequences. All images were co-registered to the MNI152 template (1mm³ resolution) and skull-stripped, resulting in standardized NIFTI format with dimensions 182×218×182. Expert-validated manual segmentation masks were created with FLAIR as the primary modality, supplemented by T2-w and T1-w sequences for ambiguous cases. The dataset includes patients with different MS subtypes (RRMS, SPMS, PPMS) and varying disease stages.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate segmentation of MS lesions is crucial for monitoring disease progression, assessing treatment efficacy, and quantifying lesion burden. Automated segmentation can reduce inter-rater variability and labor-intensive manual annotation in clinical practice.
